license: MIT

# MSBuild Item Management Patterns

Canonical patterns for working with item groups, from `Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets`.

## Include / Remove / Update вҖ” Three Operations

| Operation | Purpose | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| `Include` | Add new items to the group | Creating items with identity + metadata |
| `Remove` | Remove items matching a pattern | Excluding files or clearing a group |
| `Update` | Modify metadata on existing items | Adding/changing metadata without re-adding |

### Include вҖ” Add Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="Generated\*.cs">
    <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
  </Compile>
</ItemGroup>
```

### Remove вҖ” Subtract Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Remove specific items -->
  <Reference Remove="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)" />

  <!-- Set subtraction: prior minus current -->
  <_CleanOrphanFileWrites Include="@(_CleanPriorFileWrites)"
      Exclude="@(_CleanCurrentFileWrites)" />

  <!-- Clear an entire group -->
  <_Temporary Remove="@(_Temporary)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Update вҖ” Modify Existing Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="@(EmbeddedResource)"
      Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections'">
    <GenerateSource>true</GenerateSource>
    <ClassName>Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections.SR</ClassName>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

`Update` does not add items вҖ” it only modifies items already in the group.

## Remove + Re-Include вҖ” Replace with New Metadata

When you need to change identity or replace metadata entirely:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Reference Remove="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)" />
  <Reference Include="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)">
    <Implicit>true</Implicit>
  </Reference>
</ItemGroup>
```

## Item Batching вҖ” %(Metadata)

When `%(Metadata)` appears in target attributes or task parameters, MSBuild **batches** execution per unique metadata value.

### Target-level batching (Outputs)

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateSatelliteAssemblies"
    Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(_SatelliteAssemblyResourceInputs)"
    Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Culture)\$(TargetName).resources.dll">
  <!-- Runs once per unique Culture value -->
</Target>
```

### Task-level batching

```xml
<Copy SourceFiles="@(_SourceItems)"
    DestinationFiles="@(_SourceItems->'$(OutDir)%(TargetPath)')">
</Copy>
```

### Per-item filtering with Condition

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <_ResxOutput Include="@(EmbeddedResource->'%(OutputResource)')"
      Condition="'%(EmbeddedResource.WithCulture)' == 'false'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Batching rules

- `%(Metadata)` in `Condition` or `Outputs` вҶ’ target batches per unique value.
- `%(Metadata)` in task parameters вҶ’ task batches per unique value.
- **Do not mix `%()` from different item groups** in the same expression вҖ” this causes a cross-product (see Common Pitfalls).

## Item Transforms вҖ” @(Item->'expression')

Transforms create new item lists by applying an expression to each item:

```xml
<!-- Transform file paths to destinations -->
<Copy SourceFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly)"
    DestinationFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly->'$(OutDir)%(Filename)%(Extension)')"/>

<!-- Transform with separator for display -->
<Message Text="Files: @(Compile->'%(Filename)', ', ')" />
```

### Built-in metadata on all items

| Metadata | Value |
|---|---|
| `%(Identity)` | The item spec (full Include value) |
| `%(Filename)` | File name without extension |
| `%(Extension)` | File extension including dot |
| `%(Directory)` | Directory path |
| `%(FullPath)` | Absolute path |
| `%(RecursiveDir)` | Part matched by `**` in a glob |

## Exclude Pattern вҖ” Set Subtraction on Include

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="**\*.cs" Exclude="Generated\**;Tests\**" />
</ItemGroup>
```

`Exclude` only works on `Include` вҖ” it cannot be used with `Update` or `Remove`.

## Conditional Item Inclusion

```xml
<!-- Condition on ItemGroup вҖ” all or nothing -->
<ItemGroup Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'">
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines" />
</ItemGroup>

<!-- Condition on individual items -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines"
      Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## PrivateAssets on Tool/Analyzer Packages

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Update="Microsoft.NETCore.App" PrivateAssets="All" />
  <PackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## AdditionalFiles for Analyzers

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <AdditionalFiles Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)BannedSymbols.txt"
      Condition="'$(IsUnitTestProject)' != 'true'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## Common Pitfalls

### Cross-product batching

Referencing `%(Metadata)` from two different item groups creates O(NГ—M) executions:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Cross-product of @(Source) Г— @(Config) -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with %(Config.Identity)" />

<!-- GOOD: Reference one group via batching, the other via property -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with $(ConfigFile)" />
```

### Generated files in source tree

Write to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (obj/), not the source directory. Source-tree generation pollutes version control and can cause duplicate compilation via globs.

### Missing FileWrites

Every file created during a target must be added to `@(FileWrites)` for `dotnet clean` support.

data.content:
Here is the full review of `ItemManagement.csproj`:

---

## MSBuild Project File Review: `ItemManagement.csproj`

### рҹ”ҙ Error 1 вҖ” `Include` instead of `Update` creates duplicate `Compile` items (Lines 8вҖ“11)

```xml
<Compile Include="**\*.cs">
  <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
</Compile>
```

**Problem:** The SDK-style project (`Microsoft.NET.Sdk`) already implicitly includes all `**\*.cs` files via its default glob. Using `Include` here adds every `.cs` file **a second time** to the `Compile` item group, causing the compiler to see duplicate source files. This will produce build errors like:

> `CSC : error CS2002: Source file 'Placeholder.cs' specified multiple times`

**Fix:** Use `Update` to modify metadata on items the SDK already included:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Update="**\*.cs">
    <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
  </Compile>
</ItemGroup>
```

> **Note:** Marking *every* `.cs` file as `AutoGen="true"` is also suspicious вҖ” this metadata is typically only applied to genuinely auto-generated files. Consider narrowing the glob (e.g., `Update="**\*.g.cs"`) or removing this `ItemGroup` entirely if the metadata isn't consumed by anything.

---

### рҹ”ҙ Error 2 вҖ” Cross-product batching in `ValidateData` target (Lines 22вҖ“26)

```xml
<Message Text="Validating %(DataFile.Filename) against %(SchemaFile.Filename)"
         Importance="high" />
```

**Problem:** This references `%(Metadata)` from **two different item groups** (`DataFile` and `SchemaFile`) in the same task parameter. MSBuild creates a cross-product: every `DataFile` Г— every `SchemaFile`. With 2 data files and 2 schema files, this produces **4 messages** instead of the intended 2:

| Iteration | DataFile | SchemaFile |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | users | users |
| 2 | users | orders |
| 3 | orders | users |
| 4 | orders | orders |

The intent is clearly to validate each data file against its *matching* schema (usersвҶ”users, ordersвҶ”orders).

**Fix:** Attach the schema as metadata on `DataFile`, so batching iterates over a single item group:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <DataFile Include="data\users.json">
    <Schema>schemas\users.xsd</Schema>
  </DataFile>
  <DataFile Include="data\orders.json">
    <Schema>schemas\orders.xsd</Schema>
  </DataFile>
</ItemGroup>

<Target Name="ValidateData" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Text="Validating %(DataFile.Filename) against %(DataFile.Schema)"
           Importance="high" />
</Target>
```

This batches over `DataFile` only вҖ” 2 iterations, each with the correct pair. The separate `SchemaFile` item group (lines 18вҖ“19) can be removed.

---

### рҹ”ҙ Error 3 вҖ” Generated file written to source tree instead of `obj/` (Lines 29вҖ“31)

```xml
<WriteLinesToFile File="$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\Constants.g.cs"
                  Lines="..." Overwrite="true" />
```

**Problem:** Writing `Constants.g.cs` to the project root directory causes two issues:

1. **Source control pollution** вҖ” the generated file gets committed/shows up as dirty in `git status`.
2. **Duplicate compilation** вҖ” the SDK's default `**\*.cs` glob will pick up the file automatically at evaluation time. Then, line 34 adds it *again* via `<Compile Include="Constants.g.cs" />`, causing a duplicate-source error (same class as Error 1).

**Fix:** Write to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (the `obj/` directory) and add the generated file to `Compile` with a link for IDE display:

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateConstants" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
  <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs"
                    Lines="namespace ItemManagement { public static class Constants { public const int Version = 1%3B } }"
                    Overwrite="true" />
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs"
             Link="Generated\Constants.g.cs" />
    <FileWrites Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Target>
```

After applying this fix, **delete the existing file** from the source tree:

```
rm Constants.g.cs
```

And add `Constants.g.cs` to `.gitignore` as a safety net (in case anyone accidentally regenerates to the root).

---

### рҹҹЎ Warning 1 вҖ” Missing `FileWrites` registration (Lines 33вҖ“36)

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="Constants.g.cs" />
  <!-- Missing FileWrites registration -->
</ItemGroup>
```

**Problem:** The generated `Constants.g.cs` is never added to `@(FileWrites)`. This means `dotnet clean` won't delete it, leaving stale generated files around. This is a tracked concern even in the file's own comment on line 35.

**Fix:** Already addressed in the Error 3 fix above вҖ” `<FileWrites Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs" />` registers it for cleanup.

---

### рҹҹЎ Warning 2 вҖ” `GenerateConstants` target missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` (Line 29)

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateConstants" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
```

**Problem:** This target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so it runs on **every build** even when nothing changed. Since it generates a file with static content, this forces a recompile every time (the file timestamp updates вҶ’ Roslyn sees a changed input вҶ’ recompiles everything).

**Fix:** Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` for incremental build support. Since the content is static (no file input drives it), use the project file itself as the input:

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateConstants"
        BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFile)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs">
```

Now the target only re-runs when the `.csproj` itself changes.

---

### рҹ”ө Suggestion 1 вҖ” Remove now-unnecessary `SchemaFile` items (Lines 18вҖ“19)

If you adopt the fix for Error 2 (schema as metadata on `DataFile`), the standalone `SchemaFile` items become dead code:

```xml
<!-- Remove these two lines -->
<SchemaFile Include="schemas\users.xsd" />
<SchemaFile Include="schemas\orders.xsd" />
```

---

### рҹҹў Positive вҖ” Things done well

- **SDK-style project format** вҖ” modern and clean.
- **`BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"`** on `GenerateConstants` вҖ” correct hook point for code generation (ensures files exist before the compiler runs).
- **`Overwrite="true"`** on `WriteLinesToFile` вҖ” prevents appending to stale content.
- **Explicit `TargetFramework` and `OutputType`** вҖ” clear and intentional.

---

## Summary

| Severity | Count | Key Theme |
|---|---|---|
| рҹ”ҙ Error | 3 | Duplicate items, cross-product batching, source-tree generation |
| рҹҹЎ Warning | 2 | Missing `FileWrites`, missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` |
| рҹ”ө Suggestion | 1 | Dead item group cleanup |
| рҹҹў Positive | 4 | Modern SDK format, correct target hook |

### Consolidated Fixed File

For reference, here is what the corrected `.csproj` would look like with all fixes applied:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Use Update (not Include) to set metadata on SDK-globbed items -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Update="**\*.g.cs">
      <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
    </Compile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Pair data files with their schemas via metadata -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <DataFile Include="data\users.json">
      <Schema>schemas\users.xsd</Schema>
    </DataFile>
    <DataFile Include="data\orders.json">
      <Schema>schemas\orders.xsd</Schema>
    </DataFile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <Target Name="ValidateData" BeforeTargets="Build">
